derivation: Each D3 tier3a_cases.json case is re-derived through the same successor delta rules as the acceptance corpus (Task 6). Twelve of thirteen carry through unchanged; only uvx-continuation flips from D3 uninspectable to certified because the real Bash parser joins the backslash-newline command continuation the D3 heuristic refused.

cases.derivation: Plain doc-lattice head with the ci audit subcommand resolves directly under a traversed argv.
Plain doc-lattice check resolves directly under a traversed argv.
Plain doc-lattice lint resolves directly under a traversed argv.
The uvx launcher with --python and --from==2.0.0 resolves to doc-lattice ci audit; all option values are statically known literals, so the invocation certifies.

Successor improvement (S3.1): the real Bash parser joins the backslash-newline command continuation, so the two physical lines form one uvx doc-lattice ci audit command that certifies. Flips from the D3 recording of uninspectable / unsupported-operator.
uv run doc-lattice --help carries no subcommand invocation; the source certifies with no retained invocation.
uv run --no-sync is a supported launcher form resolving to doc-lattice check.
uv tool run is a supported launcher long form resolving to doc-lattice check.
doc-lattice check --config "$CFG": the quoted "$CFG" is single=True (one argv entry) and is the value of a non-policy option, so the check invocation certifies with dry_run false.
S3.2 traverses both && operands of the BinaryCmd; doc-lattice check and doc-lattice lint each certify.
S3.2 traverses the || operands; doc-lattice lint certifies and the exit 1 fallback carries no marker.
The offline template block resolves three sequential uvx doc-lattice invocations (ci audit, check, lint), each with statically known launcher option values.

The head word "$RUNNER_TEMP/doc-lattice-venv/bin/doc-lattice" carries a dynamic parameter, so its final text is not statically known (text=None); S5.2 refuses unstable-first-word. Unchanged from the D3 recording.
